title: 10 best herbal essential oils for migraine
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for migraine are a natural remedy that help alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing inflammation, calming the mind, and relaxing the body. The best herbal essential oils for migraine include Lavandula angustifolia, Foeniculum vulgare, Zingiber officinale, Rosmarinus officinalis, Mentha x piperita, Boswellia serrata, Salvia officinalis, Valeriana officinalis, Eucalyptus globulus, and Melissa officinalis. If left untreated, migraines can severely impact daily life, causing debilitating pain, nausea, and sensitivity to light and sound, leading to missed work, social isolation, and decreased quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi essential oils helps with migraine because of its potent anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective properties. The oil's active compounds, such as saponins and flavonoids, work to reduce inflammation in the brain, which is a common trigger for migraines. Additionally, Brahmi's adaptogenic properties help to balance the body's energy and calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can contribute to migraine episodes. As a result, using Brahmi essential oil topically or inhaling its aroma may provide relief from migraine symptoms by addressing these underlying triggers.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Brahmi essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and pain associated with migraine attacks, providing relief from discomfort and tension.

Antioxidant properties: Brahmi essential oil's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress, which is often a underlying factor in migraines, thereby reducing the frequency and severity of episodes.

Anxiolytic and stress-reducing properties: Brahmi essential oil's anxiolytic (anxiety-relieving) and adaptogenic properties help reduce mental tension, anxiety, and stress, all common migraine triggers, promoting relaxation and calmness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and other bioactive compounds that help alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing inflammation and improving blood circulation.
Rhizomes: Contain a high concentration of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing migraine pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh bacopa monnieri leaves and flowers from a healthy plant for use.
Steam distill the harvested material at 100-120°c for 4-6 hours to extract oil.
Collect the extracted oil in a clean glass container and filter it through cheesecloth.
Add 10% of sweet almond oil as carrier oil and gently blend well to mix thoroughly.
Store the bacopa monnieri essential oil in a dark glass bottle at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew essential oils helps with migraine because it contains parthenolide, a natural compound that reduces inflammation and constriction of blood vessels in the brain. This can help to alleviate migraine symptoms such as pain, sensitivity to light, and nausea. The anti-inflammatory properties of feverfew oil also help to relax tense muscles and reduce pain transmission to the brain, providing relief from throbbing headaches. By incorporating feverfew essential oils into a massage or inhalation therapy, individuals may experience reduced frequency and severity of migraines.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Feverfew essential oil reduces inflammation, which is a common underlying factor in migraines, by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals and reducing swelling in blood vessels.
Antispasmodic: Feverfew essential oil helps to relax muscles and reduce spasms, which can contribute to migraine pain and tension headaches.
Antioxidant: Feverfew essential oil contains antioxidants that help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, both of which have been linked to the development of migraines.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are rich in parthenolide, a key compound responsible for feverfew's anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Leaves: Feverfew leaves contain a high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate migraine symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest tanacetum parthenium leaves at dawn and dry them in a warm, dry place for 2-3 days.
Crush dried leaves into small pieces using mortar and pestle then mix with carrier oil such as olive or coconut oil.
Steep the mixture in a clean glass jar at room temperature for 21 days allowing it to infuse slowly.
Filter the infused mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a new glass container discarding solids and separating oils.
Store the resulting essential oil in a dark glass bottle with tight-fitting lid at cool room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with migraine because it has natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can effectively alleviate pain and tension in the head and neck. When applied topically, peppermint oil's cooling sensation can help to relax tense muscles and improve blood flow, reducing inflammation and pressure that may trigger migraines. Additionally, its calming aroma can also soothe the mind and body, promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels, a common migraine trigger.
remedy_properties: Cooing and Relaxation: Peppermint's menthol content helps to cool and relax the mind and body, reducing tension and stress that can trigger migraines.
Pain Relief: The analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties of peppermint essential oil can help to alleviate pain associated with migraines, including headaches, neck and facial pain.
Digestive Soothing: Peppermint's calming effects on the digestive system can help to ease tension and cramping in the neck and head, common symptoms of migraines.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, the roots of peppermint help to relax muscles and reduce inflammation, making them a great choice for migraine relief.
Buds: The buds of peppermint contain high levels of menthol, which helps to ease tension headaches and reduce pain associated with migraines.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from a mature plant and rinse them gently under running water.
Steam distill the peppermint leaves in a laboratory setting using a steam distillation apparatus for 3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from any impurities or contaminants immediately.
Filter the essential oil through a glass wool filter paper to remove any remaining particles or sediment for 30 minutes.
Store the mentha x piperita essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with migraine because it has a calming effect on the body, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety. The scent of lavender has been shown to promote relaxation and ease tension in the muscles and scalp, which are common triggers for migraines. Additionally, lavender's natural anti-inflammatory properties may also help to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with migraines, providing relief from symptoms such as throbbing headaches and sensitivity to light and sound.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: English Lavender essential oil's antispasmodic properties can help to relieve migraine pain by reducing muscle contractions and spasms, which are often a contributing factor to migraine episodes.

Anti-inflammatory properties: English Lavender essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and brain, which is a common underlying cause of migraines.

Anxiolytic properties: English Lavender essential oil's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties can help to calm stress and anxiety, which are common migraine triggers, reducing the frequency and severity of migraine episodes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are good for making medicinal essential oils for migraine because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help relieve migraine pain.
Flowers: Lavender flowers are a popular choice for making essential oils due to their calming effects, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that often trigger migraines.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh lavender flowers from a sunny spot at dawn or late afternoon.
Dry the harvested lavender flowers in a single layer on paper towels for 10-14 days.
Combine 50g of dried lavender flowers with 500ml of carrier oil and heat mixture in a double boiler for 4 hours.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding solids after 30 minutes.
Allow the infused oil to mature at room temperature for 21 days before decanting into dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Geranium essential oils helps with migraine because of its natural ability to reduce inflammation and ease tension in the body. The oil's fragrance has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate stress and anxiety triggers that often contribute to migraines. Additionally, geranium oil has been shown to improve circulation, which may help reduce blood vessel spasms and constrictions that are common migraine symptoms. As a result, using geranium essential oils may provide relief from migraine pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Helps to relax muscle spasms and reduce tension, which is a common trigger for migraines.
Anti-inflammatory property: Reduces inflammation in the body, which has been linked to migraine headaches, and helps alleviate pain and swelling associated with the condition.
Calming and balancing property: Has a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to calm stress and anxiety that can contribute to migraine triggers and frequency.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help alleviate migraine pain.
Flowers: Contain citral, a natural vasodilator that can help relax blood vessels and reduce migraine symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of pelargonium graveolens at dawn on a sunny day, ensuring minimal bruising or damage.
Dry the harvested leaves in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days, or until they reach a brittle consistency.
Steam distill the dried pelargonium graveolens leaves with water at a ratio of 1:4 using a clevenger apparatus, processing for 2 hours.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter paper, discarding any impurities or sediment, yielding approximately 0.5% oil content.
Transfer the filtered essential oil to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, storing in a cool, dry place for up to 1 year while keeping it away from direct sunlight and heat sources.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with migraine because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. When inhaled or applied topically, sage oil can help to relax tense muscles and reduce inflammation in the brain, which are common triggers for migraines. Additionally, sage's calming effects on the nervous system can help to ease stress and anxiety, two common migraine precursors. By reducing pain, inflammation, and stress, sage essential oils can provide natural relief from migraine symptoms, helping to alleviate discomfort and promote a sense of well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sage oil's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce swelling and irritation in blood vessels, which can contribute to migraine pain.
Antioxidant: Sage oil's antioxidant properties can help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate oxidative stress, a known trigger of migraines.
Analgesic and Sedative: Sage oil's analgesic (pain-relieving) and sedative properties may help alleviate migraine-related pain and discomfort by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have been traditionally used to relieve migraines and other headaches due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, which is a type of underground stem, contains bioactive compounds that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with migraines.
Barks: Sage barks have been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat various ailments, including headaches and migraines, due to their antispasmodic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sclarea sclarea leaves and flowers at dawn on a sunny day when dew is still present.
Steam-distill the harvested plant material using a still or a distillation apparatus with water at a ratio of 1:5.
Collect the resulting essential oil in a glass container and filter it through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities.
Store the salvia sclarea essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for up to one year.
Use the essential oil in aromatherapy by adding 5-7 drops to a diffuser or inhaling directly from the bottle for 3-5 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with migraine because of its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The oil's active compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the brain, which is a common trigger for migraines. Additionally, thyme essential oil has been found to relax tense muscles and improve blood flow, helping to alleviate symptoms of tension and stress that often accompany migraines. By using thyme essential oil in aromatherapy or topically, individuals can experience natural relief from migraine discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anticonvulsant: Thyme essential oil has been shown to have anticonvulsant properties, which can help prevent and reduce migraine frequency by reducing muscle spasms and nerve irritation.
Anti-inflammatory: Thyme essential oil contains compounds like thymol and carvacrol that have potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate pain and swelling associated with migraines.
Cephalic analgesic: Thyme essential oil has been traditionally used to relieve headaches and migraines due to its ability to stimulate blood flow to the head and scalp, reducing pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which are known to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in relieving migraine pain.
Flowers: The flowers of the thyme plant are rich in flavonoids and volatile oils, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate migraine symptoms such as headache and nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thyme leaves and stems, washing them thoroughly with distilled water to remove impurities.
Steam distill the thyme mixture using 20g of plant material per 100ml of distilled water for 4 hours.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil, separating it from the aqueous phase within 30 minutes.
Transfer the extracted essential oil to dark glass bottles filled with carrier oil at a ratio of 5% thymus vulgaris essential oil to 95% carrier oil.
Store the herbal blend in a cool, dry place for a minimum of 2 weeks before use to allow flavors and properties to mature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with migraine because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling in blood vessels and ease muscle tension. The oil's analgesic and sedative properties also work together to relieve pain and promote relaxation, helping to calm the body and mind. Additionally, ginger has natural pain-relieving compounds called gingerols and shogaols, which can help to block the production of pain-causing neurotransmitters in the brain.
remedy_properties: Pain relief: Ginger essential oil's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate migraines by reducing pain and inflammation in the body, providing temporary relief from throbbing headaches.

Digestive aid: Ginger essential oil's carminative and antispasmodic properties can help soothe digestive issues that may contribute to migraine triggers, such as nausea and stomach discomfort, by reducing spasms and gas buildup in the digestive tract.

Anti-inflammatory: Ginger essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels of the brain, which is a common underlying cause of migraines, thereby helping to prevent and alleviate symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which help to relax muscles and reduce inflammation associated with migraines.
Rhyzomes: High in volatile compounds that have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making them effective against migraine symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger roots with their skin intact, cutting them into small pieces.
Steam distill the ginger pieces for 4 hours or until the oil yield reaches 20%.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it using a coffee filter paper.
Mix the filtered essential oil with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil at a ratio of 1:10.
Store the prepared herbal zingiber officinale essential oil in a dark glass bottle for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian essential oils helps with migraine because of its calming and sedative properties. The oil's ability to reduce anxiety, stress, and tension headaches makes it a popular natural remedy for migraines. When inhaled or applied topically, valerian essential oil can help relax the mind and body, reducing the frequency and severity of migraine attacks. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties may also help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with migraines. As a result, incorporating valerian essential oils into your self-care routine may provide relief from these debilitating headaches.
remedy_properties: Sedative and calming effects: Valerian essential oil has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help to calm and relax the mind and body, reducing the frequency and severity of migraines caused by stress and anxiety.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to reduce swelling and pain in the brain and scalp, which are common symptoms of migraine headaches.

Muscle relaxation and analgesic effects: Valerian essential oil's ability to relax muscles and relieve pain can help to alleviate migraine-related tension headaches and reduce the intensity of migraines caused by muscle contractions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are rich in sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to alleviate migraine symptoms.
Flowers: The fragrant flowers of valerian contain a high concentration of essential oil, which has anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic properties that may help relieve migraine pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh valeriana officinalis roots and stems on a sunny day.
Dry the harvested material in a warm, dark place for 7-10 days or until crumbly.
Place 30g of dried valeriana officinalis into a clean glass jar and cover with 200ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool, dark place for 14-21 days, shaking occasionally.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard solids, transferring the infused oil to a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with migraine because it contains a unique combination of compounds that work synergistically to reduce pain and inflammation. The oil's terpenes, such as caryophyllene and alpha-pinene, have potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate headache pain and discomfort. Additionally, the oil's analgesic and sedative properties can help to calm the mind and body, providing relief from the intense sensory disturbances associated with migraines. This natural remedy has been shown to provide fast and effective relief for those suffering from this debilitating condition.
remedy_properties: Pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory properties: Eucalyptus globulus contains eucalyptol, which has been shown to reduce pain and inflammation, providing relief from migraine symptoms.
Antispasmodic properties: The oil's ability to relax muscle spasms can help alleviate tension headaches that often accompany migraines, reducing the frequency and severity of episodes.

Cooling and decongestant properties: Eucalyptus globulus essential oil has a refreshing and cooling effect on the body, which can help to reduce sinus pressure and congestion that may exacerbate migraine symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to help alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing inflammation and relaxing blood vessels.
Barks: The barks of the Tasmanian Blue Gum plant contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help relieve migraine pain and reduce inflammation in the brain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves and wash them gently with distilled water.
Dry the leaves in a warm oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 4 hours.
Steam distill 10 grams of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in 100 milliliters of distilled water for 2 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil using cheesecloth and store it in dark glass bottles at room temperature.
Dilute the essential oil with carrier oils such as coconut or olive oil at a ratio of 10 percent essential oil to 90 percent carrier oil.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat migraine with herbal essential oils is to blend Lavender, Peppermint, and Eucalyptus oils in a carrier oil like coconut or sweet almond oil. Apply the mixture to the temples, forehead, and neck to ease pain and tension. Additionally, inhale the vapors by adding a few drops of the blend to a diffuser or inhaling directly from a cloth or handkerchief. This holistic approach can provide fast and effective relief from migraine symptoms.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to migraine that are treated with herbal essential oils are tension headaches, facial pain, and sinusitis. Lavender oil is often used to ease stress and promote relaxation, while peppermint oil helps to cool and soothe the skin. Eucalyptus oil is commonly used to relieve sinus pressure and congestion, while geranium oil helps to balance hormones and reduce menstrual cramps. These herbal essential oils can be inhaled, applied topically, or added to bath water for relief from these ailments.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with migraine because of its calming and analgesic properties. The oil's primary constituents, linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to have a positive effect on reducing inflammation and relaxing tense muscles, which are common triggers for migraines. Additionally, the oil's sedative properties can help regulate sleep patterns and reduce stress levels, which can also contribute to migraine relief. As a result, Lavandula angustifolia essential oils can be a valuable tool in managing and preventing migraines.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Acts as an anxiolytic, reducing stress and anxiety that often trigger migraines.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate migraine symptoms such as pain and inflammation.
Caryophyllene oxide: Possesses analgesic and antispasmodic properties, helping to reduce pain and muscle tension associated with migraines.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in linalool and linalyl acetate, the primary compounds that contribute to the calming and anti-inflammatory properties of lavender essential oil.
Flowers: Contain high concentrations of linalool and linalyl acetate, which are known to have a sedative effect and can help reduce migraine symptoms.

Stems: Although less commonly used, the stems of Lavandula angustifolia contain some of the same compounds as the flowers and leaves, which can contribute to the oil's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 kilogram of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Steam distill the flowers with 5 liters of water at 100 degrees celsius for 3 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the water using a distillation apparatus and collect 50 milliliters of oil.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter to remove any impurities and contaminants.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare essential oils helps with migraine because of its natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The oil contains compounds such as anethole and fenchone, which have been shown to relax muscles and reduce pain. Additionally, its sedative properties can help alleviate stress and anxiety, common triggers for migraines. The oil's ability to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation can help provide relief from migraine symptoms, making it a potential natural remedy for those suffering from this condition. Regular use may also help prevent future episodes.
remedy_constituents: Anethole: Helps to reduce migraine pain and inflammation by acting as an analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent.
Fenchone: Exhibits analgesic and spasmolytic properties, which help in relieving migraine pain and reducing muscle spasms associated with migraine headaches.
Linalool: Demonstrates analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective properties, which help in alleviating migraine pain, reducing inflammation, and protecting the nervous system from damage.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used because they contain a high concentration of volatile oils, particularly anethole, which has been shown to have potential migraine-relieving properties.
Seeds: They are used because the seeds of Fennel contain a higher concentration of essential oils than the leaves, including anethole, which may help alleviate migraine symptoms.
Stems: They are used because the stems of Fennel contain a unique blend of essential oils, including anethole, which has been traditionally used to help manage migraine and other types of headaches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare seeds or dried flowers from mature plants in late summer or early fall.
Steam distill 100 grams of foeniculum vulgare material with 3 liters of distilled water for 3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separator funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter or a coffee filter to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the foeniculum vulgare essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with migraine because of its potent anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. The oil contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which work to reduce inflammation in blood vessels and alleviate migraine symptoms. Additionally, ginger has natural sedative properties that can help calm the nervous system and promote relaxation, further reducing migraine severity. Its ability to ease nausea and vomiting associated with migraines makes it a promising natural remedy for managing this debilitating condition.
remedy_constituents: Bisabolol: Helps with migraine by reducing inflammation and pain due to its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Gingerols: Assist in alleviating migraine symptoms by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which contribute to pain and inflammation.
Zingiberene: Eases migraine discomfort by possessing anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, thereby reducing pain and discomfort associated with migraines.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Zingiber officinale due to their high concentration of active compounds, including gingerols and shogaols, which have potent anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Roots: Roots are also widely used for their bioactive compounds, particularly gingerols and shogaols, which help alleviate migraine symptoms.
Buds: Buds are used for their high concentration of volatile oils, including gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, making them effective in managing migraine pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 3-4 kilos of fresh zingiber officinale roots in the early morning when the dew is still present.
Wash the roots thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and impurities from the surface gently.
Steam distill the roots in a 100-liter still for 2-3 hours at 100 degrees celsius temperature carefully.
Separate the essential oil from the distillate using a glass separator and store it in airtight containers immediately.
Filter the essential oil through a 10-micron filter paper to remove any remaining impurities and debris completely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils helps with migraine because of its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The oil's ability to ease tension and promote relaxation can also contribute to its migraine-relieving effects. Additionally, the oil's antioxidant properties may help reduce oxidative stress, a factor that can contribute to migraine development. By reducing inflammation, easing tension, and combating oxidative stress, Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils can help alleviate migraine symptoms and provide relief for those affected. Its natural properties make it a popular alternative remedy.
remedy_constituents: Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the pain and inflammation associated with migraine.
Bornyl acetate: This sesquiterpene has analgesic and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate the pain and muscle spasms associated with migraine.
Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic acid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the inflammation and oxidative stress that contribute to migraine pain and frequency.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Rosmarinus officinalis are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing migraine symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers of Rosmarinus officinalis are known for their calming and relaxing effects, which can help in alleviating migraine pain and tension.
Stems: The stems of Rosmarinus officinalis contain essential oils that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce migraine pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves from mature plants in early morning to capture maximum oil content.
Steam distill the rosmarinus officinalis leaves in a still with 10 pounds of water at 212°f for 4 hours.
Collect the rosmarinus officinalis essential oil that separates from the water and has a density of 0.940-0.960 g/ml.
Filter the rosmarinus officinalis essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities and improve clarity.
Store the rosmarinus officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils helps with migraine because it has natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can provide relief from migraine symptoms. The menthol content in peppermint oil can help relax tense muscles and improve blood flow, reducing tension and discomfort. Additionally, the oil's ability to ease digestive issues, such as nausea and vomiting, associated with migraines can provide overall relief. Its cooling effect can also help to reduce pain and inflammation, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Limonene: Acts as a natural anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective agent, helping to reduce pain and inflammation associated with migraines.
Caryophyllene: Exhibits potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, contributing to the relief of migraine pain and inflammation.
Rosmarinic acid: Displays potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, helping to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that can trigger and exacerbate migraines.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Mentha x piperita are primarily used to make essential oils for migraine due to their high concentration of menthol, which provides pain relief.
Stems: The stems of Mentha x piperita are used to create essential oils that help alleviate migraine symptoms through the release of menthone, a compound that relaxes muscles.

Flowers: The flowers of Mentha x piperita are utilized to produce essential oils that help combat migraine headaches due to their high content of limonene, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mint leaves by hand at dawn to minimize oil loss and maximize potency.
Steam distill the harvested mint leaves for 2 hours to separate the essential oil from water.
Collect the distilled water and essential oil by condensing the steam into a collection vessel.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water through decantation or centrifugation to achieve purity.
Store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight-fitting lid to preserve freshness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata essential oils helps with migraine because it contains boswellic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. These compounds work to reduce inflammation and pain in the blood vessels and nerves of the brain, helping to alleviate migraine symptoms. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce the frequency and severity of migraines by preventing blood vessel constriction and promoting relaxation, leading to a reduction in migraine episodes and related pain. Regular use can provide relief.
remedy_constituents: Alpha-boswellic acid: It helps reduce inflammation and alleviate migraine symptoms by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.
Beta-boswellic acid: It exhibits anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer properties, which can help in reducing migraine pain and frequency by modulating the body's inflammatory response.

3-Acetyl-11-keto-beta-boswellic acid (AKBA): It possesses potent anti-inflammatory and anti-pain properties, which can help in reducing migraine pain and inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators.
remedy_parts: Roots: Boswellia serrata roots are the primary source of essential oils used to treat migraines due to their high concentration of boswellic acids.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of Boswellia serrata contain boswellic acids, which are extracted to create essential oils that help alleviate migraine symptoms.
Barks: The barks of Boswellia serrata are also used to produce essential oils, which contain boswellic acids that help in reducing migraine severity and frequency.
remedy_recipe: Harvest boswellia serrata resin by cutting the tree trunk at an angle to allow the resin to flow freely.
Steam distill the collected resin at 100-150c using a ratio of 1:2 resin to water for 3-4 hours.
Collect the obtained distillate and filter it using a paper filter to remove any impurities.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to prevent degradation.
Label and date the bottle and store it in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis essential oils helps with migraine because of its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The oil contains compounds like borneol and thujone, which have been shown to ease pain and reduce inflammation in the brain. Additionally, the oil's antioxidant properties help to protect the brain from oxidative stress, a known trigger for migraines. By calming the nervous system and reducing inflammation, Salvia officinalis essential oils can provide relief from migraine symptoms, promoting relaxation and reducing pain.
remedy_constituents: Carnosic acid: It helps with migraine by its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential anti-migraine effects.
Rosmarinic acid: It helps with migraine by its ability to inhibit pro-inflammatory enzymes and reduce oxidative stress, which may help alleviate migraine symptoms.
Salvianol G: It helps with migraine by its potential to inhibit pain pathways and reduce inflammation, although more research is needed to fully understand its mechanisms of action.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of essential oil in Salvia officinalis, known for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate migraine pain.
Stems: The stems of Salvia officinalis contain essential oils that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing pain and inflammation associated with migraines.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves in the morning when the dew is still present for maximum oil quality.
Clean the leaves thoroughly with distilled water to remove any dirt or impurities from the surface.
Steam distill the leaves at 100-110°c for 2-3 hours to release the essential oil from plant cells.
Collect the essential oil by condensing the steam distillation process using a cooled condenser coil.
Filter the essential oil using a paper or cotton filter to remove any remaining plant particles and debris.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis essential oils helps with migraine because it contains valerenic acid, a natural compound that has a sedative effect on the nervous system. This helps to reduce muscle tension and alleviate migraine symptoms such as pain and sensitivity to light and sound. Additionally, its calming properties can help to regulate the body's stress response, which is often a trigger for migraines. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, Valeriana officinalis essential oils can provide relief from migraine episodes.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Acts as a GABA receptor agonist, which helps to reduce anxiety and stress that can trigger migraines.
Isovaleric acid: Exhibits analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, providing relief from migraine pain and inflammation.
Valeranone: Possesses a sedative effect, helping to relax muscles and promote better sleep, which can be beneficial in reducing migraine frequency and severity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are the primary source of valerenic acid, a key compound responsible for the sedative and anti-inflammatory effects that help alleviate migraine symptoms.
Leaves: Leaves are rich in valepotriates, which contribute to the oil's ability to calm the nervous system and reduce migraine pain.
Flowers: Flowers contain valerenic acid and other compounds that help to relax muscles, reduce inflammation, and ease migraine discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots and stems at dawn when the plant is at its highest moisture content.
Steam distill the harvested plant material in a ratio of 1 part plant to 3 parts water for 2 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separator or decanter.
Filter the essential oil through a 5-micron filter to remove any remaining impurities and debris.
Store the final valeriana officinalis essential oil in a dark glass container away from heat and light sources.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils helps with migraine because it has natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can provide relief from headache pain and stiffness. The menthol content in Eucalyptus globulus oil has a cooling effect, which can help constrict blood vessels and reduce inflammation in the brain. Additionally, its decongestant properties can help ease sinus pressure, a common migraine trigger. By applying Eucalyptus globulus oil topically or inhaling it through steam inhalation, individuals may experience a reduction in migraine frequency and severity.
remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol: It has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help alleviate migraine pain and reduce inflammation in the brain.
Cineole: It has a vasodilatory effect, which helps to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow to the brain, thereby reducing migraine symptoms.
Bornyl acetate: It has a sedative and antispasmodic effect, which helps to calm the nervous system, reduce muscle tension, and alleviate migraine headaches.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Eucalyptus globulus leaves are the most commonly used part to make essential oils for migraine relief due to their high eucalyptol content, which provides analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.

Barks: Eucalyptus globulus barks are another key part used in essential oil production for migraines, as they contain a high concentration of eucalyptol and other compounds that help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation.

Stems: Eucalyptus globulus stems are also used in making essential oils for migraines, as they contain eucalyptol and other bioactive compounds that contribute to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 kg of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves from mature trees, ideally early morning to ensure peak oil content.
Steam distill the harvested leaves in a 20-liter distillation apparatus for 2-3 hours to release the essential oil.
Separate the essential oil from the distillation apparatus by collecting the oil that condenses on the cooling coils over 1 hour.
Filter the collected essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter to remove any impurities or debris over 30 minutes.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool, dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oils helps with migraine because of its calming and soothing properties. The oil contains compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects. These properties can help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with migraines. Additionally, Melissa officinalis oil has a relaxing effect on the mind, reducing stress and anxiety that often trigger migraines. By promoting a sense of calm and relaxation, Melissa officinalis oil may help to prevent and alleviate migraine symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Limonene: Helps with migraine by reducing inflammation and modulating the activity of neurotransmitters involved in pain perception.
Linalool: Contributes to migraine relief by acting as a sedative and anxiolytic, which can help alleviate stress and anxiety that often trigger or exacerbate migraines.
Rosmarinic acid: Assists in migraine relief by acting as an antioxidant, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, potentially contributing to migraine pathophysiology.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of rosmarinic acid, which is known for its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Stems: They are used because they contain essential oils that help in reducing stress, anxiety, and pain associated with migraines.
Flowers: They are used for their calming and soothing effects, which help in alleviating migraine symptoms and promoting relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 lbs of melissa officinalis leaves at the peak of their fragrance when the flowers are in full bloom.
Steam distill the melissa officinalis leaves for 4-6 hours to extract the essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separator.
Filter the essential oil through a paper or cloth filter to remove any impurities and debris.
Store the melissa officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.
